engineGuide.h2: Volvo XC90 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Volvo XC90 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: The iconic D5 inline‑five diesel that defined the first‑gen XC90, bringing strong torque and respectable economy - though EGR and injector issues later drove replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: D5, D5 AWD, D5 SE, D5 SE Lux, D5 Momentum, D5 R-Design, D5 Inscription
commonFailures: EGR cooler cracking causing coolant loss (60k-100k miles)
Injector seal failure leading to fuel dilution
Swirl flap motor failure on intake manifold
entries.cta: Get quotes for D5244T Volvo XC90 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: Updated D5 variant with revised turbo and EGR calibration for improved emissions compliance in later first‑generation XC90 models.
compatibleVariants: D5 AWD, D5 SE, D5 SE Lux, D5 R-Design
commonFailures: Turbo wastegate actuator sticking
DPF regeneration issues on short‑journey use
Vacuum pump failure affecting brake servo
entries.cta: Get quotes for D5244T4 Volvo XC90 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: Final evolution of the first‑gen D5 with Euro 5 emissions, featuring refined common‑rail injection and improved reliability over earlier codes.
compatibleVariants: D3, D5, D5 AWD, D5 Momentum, D5 Inscription
commonFailures: High‑pressure fuel pump wear causing hard starts
EGR valve carbon buildup reducing power
Timing belt tensioner wear (interference engine)
entries.cta: Get quotes for D5244T5 Volvo XC90 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: Late‑production D5 code with updated engine management for improved fuel economy and reduced emissions in final first‑generation XC90s.
compatibleVariants: D5 AWD, D5 Inscription, D5 R-Design
commonFailures: AdBlue system faults on Euro 6 conversions
Turbo oil feed coking at high mileage
Glow plug relay failure in cold climates
entries.cta: Get quotes for D5244T18 Volvo XC90 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Volvo's compact Drive‑E four‑cylinder diesel bringing improved efficiency and lower emissions to the second‑generation XC90, though turbo reliability remains a concern.
compatibleVariants: D4, D4 AWD, D4 Momentum, D4 R-Design, D4 Inscription
commonFailures: Turbo wastegate actuator failure causing boost loss
EGR cooler internal leakage (coolant consumption)
Timing chain tensioner wear on high‑mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4204T14 Volvo XC90 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Updated Drive‑E D4 with refined injection strategy and improved thermal management for better real‑world economy in later second‑gen XC90s.
compatibleVariants: D4 Momentum, D4 Inscription, D4 R-Design, D4 Pro
commonFailures: SCR system dosing valve failure
Oil dilution from frequent DPF regeneration
High‑pressure pump seal leakage
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4204T23 Volvo XC90 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.9L Petrol
entries.history: Volvo's twin‑turbo inline‑six petrol offering strong performance in early XC90 T6 models, though timing chain and oil consumption issues emerged with age.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure causing valve damage
Turbo oil feed coking leading to bearing wear
PCV system failure causing oil consumption
entries.cta: Get quotes for B6294T Volvo XC90 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol
entries.history: The reliable turbo five‑cylinder petrol engine offering a balance of performance and simplicity in early XC90 2.5T models.
commonFailures: Turbocharger wastegate sticking
Ignition coil failure causing misfires
Oil sludge buildup on neglected units
entries.cta: Get quotes for B5254T2 Volvo XC90 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol
entries.history: Volvo's naturally aspirated inline‑six SI6 engine brought smoother delivery and improved reliability over the earlier twin‑turbo units in mid‑generation XC90s.
commonFailures: Variable valve timing solenoid failure
Oil consumption from piston ring wear
Intake manifold runner control faults
entries.cta: Get quotes for B6324S Volvo XC90 engine replacement

entries.title: 4.4L Petrol
entries.history: Yamaha‑designed V8 offering flagship performance in XC90 V8 models, though complexity and parts availability make replacements costly.
compatibleVariants: V8, V8 AWD, V8 Sport, V8 Executive
commonFailures: Valve guide wear causing oil consumption
Timing chain guide plastic degradation
Oil cooler gasket failure leading to leaks

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The turbocharged Drive‑E four‑cylinder petrol bringing strong performance and improved efficiency to second‑generation XC90 T5 models.
compatibleVariants: T5, T5 Momentum, T5 R-Design, T5 Inscription
commonFailures: Piston ring wear causing oil consumption
Turbocharger oil seal leakage
High‑pressure fuel pump failure

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol (Twincharged)
entries.history: Volvo's twincharged (turbo + supercharger) Drive‑E unit delivering flagship T6 performance in compact displacement for second‑gen XC90s.
compatibleVariants: T6, T6 AWD, T6 Momentum, T6 R-Design, T6 Inscription
commonFailures: Supercharger clutch failure causing loss of boost
Turbo oil feed coking at high mileage
Timing chain tensioner wear on early units

entries.title: 2.0L Hybrid
entries.history: Volvo's plug‑in hybrid powertrain combining a turbocharged Drive‑E petrol engine with electric motor for exceptional performance and reduced emissions.
compatibleVariants: T8 Twin Engine, T8 Momentum, T8 R-Design, T8 Inscription, Recharge Plus, Recharge Ultimate

Electric motor inverter failure causing limp mode

commonProblems.h2: Volvo XC90 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Volvo XC90's D5 diesel and T6 petrol engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, with EGR cooler failures and timing chain issues being the most common catalysts. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a Volvomum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your specific XC90.

problems.h4: D5 EGR Cooler Failure - Why Volvo XC90 Diesel Engines Lose Coolant
problems.affectedModels: Volvo XC90 D5, D5 AWD, D5 Momentum, D5 Inscription (2003-2014, D5244T family)
problems.rootCause: Thermal cycling causes the EGR cooler's internal welds to crack, allowing exhaust gases to contaminate the coolant system and leading to overheating or head gasket stress.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake, replace cooler assembly, flush coolant system
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-60k miles if driving patterns unchanged; suitable for low‑mileage XC90s

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cooler, valve, pipes; clean intake manifold; update ECU map
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles; recommended if cooler failed due to carbon buildup

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded EGR components, bench tested, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 80k+ miles; best value for XC90s with 100k+ miles or additional engine wear

problems.recommendation: If your XC90 D5 has under 90k miles and the failure is isolated to the EGR cooler, the intermediate service offers good value. However, if there's evidence of coolant contamination in the oil or the engine has high mileage, a rebuilt unit with upgraded components is more cost‑effective long‑term.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt D5244T engine prices for your Volvo XC90

problems.h4: T6 Timing Chain Failure - Catastrophic Risk on Early Petrol XC90s
problems.affectedModels: Volvo XC90 T6, T6 AWD (2003-2005, B6294T)
problems.rootCause: The rear‑mounted timing chain tensioner loses hydraulic pressure with age, allowing chain slack that can jump teeth and cause valve‑to‑piston contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, guides, chain; requires engine removal
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative only; not viable once noise or misfires appear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioner design, bench tested, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 80k+ miles; only realistic option once valve damage has occurred

problems.recommendation: There is no reliable "minor fix" once timing chain noise or misfires appear on the B6294T. If your XC90 T6 shows these symptoms, budget for a rebuilt engine - attempting a chain‑only repair risks catastrophic secondary damage.
problems.cta: Get quotes for B6294T Volvo XC90 replacement engines

problems.h4: Drive‑E Turbo Wastegate Failure - Boost Loss on Second‑Gen XC90s
problems.affectedModels: Volvo XC90 D4, T5, T6 (2014-2024, B4204T/D4204T family)
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup on the turbo wastegate pivot causes sticking, leading to over‑boost or under‑boost faults and eventual turbo bearing damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace electronic wastegate actuator; recalibrate boost control
repairOptions.longevity: 20k-40k miles; suitable if turbo internals are undamaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip, clean, replace bearings/seals; balance rotating assembly
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-60k miles; viable if housing is crack‑free

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo, bench tested, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 80k+ miles; best for high‑mileage XC90s or if oil contamination occurred

problems.recommendation: If boost faults appear before 70k miles and oil quality has been maintained, the actuator replacement or turbo rebuild can extend life cost‑effectively. For XC90s over 90k miles or with evidence of oil in the intake, a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo components offers better long‑term value.
problems.cta: Compare Drive‑E engine prices for your Volvo XC90

problems.h4: D5 Injector Failure - Fuel Dilution and Hard Starting
problems.affectedModels: Volvo XC90 D5, D5 AWD (2003-2014, D5244T family)
problems.rootCause: Piezo injector seals degrade, allowing diesel to leak into the cylinder liner, diluting engine oil and accelerating bearing wear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, replace copper seals and retention clips
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative only; not viable once oil dilution is confirmed

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all five injectors with coding; flush oil system
repairOptions.longevity: 30k-50k miles; suitable if caught before bearing damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded injectors, bench tested, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 80k+ miles; essential if oil analysis shows bearing material

problems.recommendation: If oil analysis confirms fuel dilution or metallic particles, do not attempt injector‑only repairs - the bearings are likely compromised. A rebuilt engine with upgraded injectors is the only reliable solution for high‑mileage D5 XC90s.
problems.cta: Get quotes for D5244T rebuilt engines for your XC90

problems.h4: V8 Valve Guide Wear - Oil Consumption on Flagship XC90s
problems.affectedModels: Volvo XC90 V8, V8 AWD (2005-2011, B8444S)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust valve guides wear prematurely due to thermal stress, allowing oil to enter the combustion chamber and causing blue smoke on overrun.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove heads, replace valve guides/seals, machine seats
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-60k miles; viable if block and bottom end are sound

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt V8 with upgraded guides, bench tested, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 80k+ miles; recommended for XC90s with additional wear or high mileage

problems.recommendation: Given the B8444S's complexity and parts cost, a full rebuilt engine often represents better value than a head‑only repair, especially if the XC90 has other age‑related wear. The rebuilt option also includes warranty protection that a head recondition cannot match.
problems.cta: Compare V8 engine replacement prices for your Volvo XC90

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Volvo XC90
finalCta.paragraph: The problems above represent the most common catalysts for XC90 engine replacement enquiries in the UK. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Volvo XC90 specialists for rebuilt, reconditioned and used engines. Every unit supplied through our network includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a Volvomum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty with nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Volvo XC90 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Volvo XC90 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a Volvomum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Volvo XC90 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Volvo XC90 is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

items.title: Volvo XC90 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L Drive‑E diesel powers second‑generation XC90 D4 models, bringing improved fuel economy and Euro 6 compliance though turbo reliability requires attention on higher‑mileage examples.
items.cta: Get quotes for Volvo XC90 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Volvo XC90 2.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The iconic 2.4L D5 inline‑five diesel defined the first‑generation XC90, offering strong torque and proven longevity though EGR and injector issues drive replacement demand on higher‑mileage units.
items.cta: Get quotes for Volvo XC90 2.4L diesel engines

items.title: Volvo XC90 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L Drive‑E petrol powers T5 and T6 second‑generation XC90 models, delivering strong performance from compact displacement though piston ring wear and turbo issues require monitoring.
commonFailurePoints: Piston ring wear causing oil consumption
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC90 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volvo XC90 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.5L turbo five‑cylinder petrol offered balanced performance in early XC90 2.5T models, with good reliability though turbo wastegate and ignition issues emerge with age.
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC90 2.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volvo XC90 2.9L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.9L twin‑turbo inline‑six powered early XC90 T6 models with strong performance, though timing chain tensioner failure remains a critical risk requiring preventative attention.
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC90 2.9L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volvo XC90 3.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.2L naturally aspirated SI6 inline‑six brought smoother delivery and improved reliability to mid‑generation XC90s, though VVT solenoid and oil consumption issues require monitoring.
commonFailurePoints: piston ring wear causing oil consumption
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC90 3.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volvo XC90 4.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.4L Yamaha‑designed V8 offered flagship performance in XC90 V8 models, though complexity, parts cost and valve guide wear make replacements a premium investment.
commonFailurePoints: Valve guide wear causing oil consumption
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC90 4.4L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Volvo XC90 Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Volvo XC90 2.0L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The 2.0L Drive‑E plug‑in hybrid combines turbo petrol with electric motor for exceptional performance and reduced emissions in T8 Twin Engine and Recharge XC90 models.
commonFailurePoints: petrol engine oil dilution from electric‑only use
items.cta: Enquire about Volvo XC90 2.0L hybrid engine availability

items.description: Diesel powers the majority of UK XC90s across both generations, with the D5 inline‑five (2003-2014) and Drive‑E four‑cylinder (2014-present) dominating replacement enquiries due to EGR and turbo issues.
items.descriptor: Diesel powers the majority of UK XC90s across both generations, with the D5 inline‑five (2003-2014) and Drive‑E four‑cylinder (2014-present) dominating replacement enquiries due to EGR and turbo issues.

D4204T14 - Drive‑E D4, improved efficiency, turbo wastegate concerns

engineYears.h2: Volvo XC90 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the XC90's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The XC90 launched with turbo five‑cylinder petrol and the iconic D5 diesel, establishing the model's premium SUV credentials in the UK market.
keyChanges: XC90 introduced as Volvo's first SUV with P2 platform underpinnings
D5244T diesel and B5254T2/B6294T petrol engines available from launch
AWD system standard on most variants for UK market
knownFor: D5 diesel establishes strong towing reputation in UK
T6 twin‑turbo performance praised but timing chain concerns emerge
Build quality and safety features drive premium positioning
Early examples now entering replacement‑engine demographic
years.cta: Get quotes for 2003-2006 Volvo XC90 replacement engines
years.ticker: Volvo XC90 D5 engine replacement • XC90 T6 timing chain failure • XC90 2.5T turbo issues

years.description: The 2007 facelift introduced the smoother 3.2L SI6 petrol and updated D5 variants, while the flagship V8 arrived for UK buyers seeking maximum performance.
keyChanges: 3.2L B6324S SI6 replaces 2.9L twin‑turbo in most markets
V8 B8444S introduced as flagship powertrain for XC90
D5244T4 updated with revised turbo and emissions calibration
knownFor: 3.2L SI6 praised for smoother delivery than twin‑turbo predecessor
V8 offers flagship performance but complex repairs drive replacement costs
D5244T4 EGR cooler issues begin appearing on higher‑mileage examples
Mid‑generation XC90s now core demographic for engine replacement
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2011 Volvo XC90 replacement engines
years.ticker: XC90 3.2 oil consumption • XC90 V8 valve guide wear • XC90 D5 EGR cooler failure

years.description: Final production years of the first‑gen XC90 feature updated D5244T5 and D5244T18 codes with Euro 5 compliance, representing the most refined versions of the iconic inline‑five diesel.
keyChanges: D5244T5 and D5244T18 introduced with Euro 5 emissions
Minor interior updates and equipment revisions
Production shifts focus to second‑generation development
knownFor: D5244T5/T18 represent most reliable first‑gen D5 variants
Injector seal and EGR issues still drive replacement demand
Strong residual values keep these XC90s on UK roads
Ideal candidates for rebuilt engine upgrades with warranty
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2014 Volvo XC90 replacement engines

years.description: The all‑new YV5 chassis XC90 arrives with modular SPA platform and Drive‑E four‑cylinder engines, bringing improved efficiency and technology but new failure patterns.
keyChanges: New SPA platform with lighter construction and improved safety
Drive‑E four‑cylinder engines replace five‑ and six‑cylinder units
T5, T6 petrol and D4 diesel available; T8 hybrid introduced 2016
knownFor: Drive‑E engines praised for efficiency but turbo reliability concerns emerge
T8 hybrid complexity creates specialist replacement requirements
Early YV5 XC90s now entering replacement‑engine demographic
UK buyers value improved technology and safety features
years.cta: Get quotes for 2014-2017 Volvo XC90 replacement engines
years.ticker: XC90 D4 turbo wastegate • XC90 T5 piston ring wear • XC90 T8 hybrid battery

years.description: Mid‑life updates bring refined Drive‑E engines with improved thermal management and the introduction of mild‑hybrid B5/B6 variants alongside continuing T8 plug‑in hybrid.
keyChanges: Mild‑hybrid B5/B6 variants introduced with 48V system
Updated infotainment and driver assistance features
D4204T23 and B4204T27 receive calibration refinements
knownFor: Mild‑hybrid systems add complexity but improve refinement
T8 Recharge offers strong performance with reduced emissions
Drive‑E turbo and piston ring issues remain key replacement drivers
Strong UK demand for supply‑and‑fit packages on these models
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2021 Volvo XC90 replacement engines
years.ticker: XC90 B5 mild hybrid issues • XC90 T8 inverter fault • XC90 D4 EGR cooler

years.description: Latest XC90 models feature refined Drive‑E powertrains with updated emissions systems and enhanced hybrid technology, though replacement demand remains focused on earlier second‑gen examples.
keyChanges: Updated exterior styling and interior technology
Enhanced hybrid battery capacity on T8 Recharge models
Continued production of D4, B5, B6 and T8 variants for UK market
knownFor: Latest examples still under warranty; replacement demand focuses on 2014-2019 models
Hybrid system expertise increasingly important for T8 replacements
Drive‑E engine codes remain consistent, aiding parts compatibility
UK specialists report strong demand for rebuilt units with warranty

faq.h2: Volvo XC90 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Volvo XC90 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Volvo XC90 engine replacement costs range from £1,450 for a rebuilt D5 diesel to £8,500 for a V8 unit, depending on engine code, condition and whether supply‑only or fitted pricing is required.
keyPoints: D5244T rebuilt engines typically £1,450-£3,200 supply only
Drive‑E B4204T units range £2,200-£5,900 depending on variant
Supply & fit adds £650-£1,400 to engine price at network specialists
All rebuilt units include Volvomum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC90 engine replacement prices for your variant

items.question: Should I repair or replace my Volvo XC90 engine?
items.answer: Replacement makes financial sense if repair costs exceed 60% of a rebuilt engine price, or if the XC90 has over 100k miles with additional wear items needing attention.
keyPoints: EGR cooler or injector repairs on D5 often cost £650-£1,400
Timing chain repairs on T6 B6294T risk catastrophic secondary damage
Rebuilt engines include upgraded components addressing original failure points
Warranty protection on rebuilt units adds long‑term value vs repair
items.cta: Get a Volvo XC90 engine replacement quote to compare options

items.question: What causes Volvo XC90 D5 engine failure?
items.answer: EGR cooler cracking and injector seal failure are the most common D5 failure causes on XC90 models, typically appearing between 70k-110k miles with coolant loss or oil dilution symptoms.
keyPoints: EGR cooler internal welds crack from thermal cycling
Injector copper seals degrade allowing fuel into crankcase
Symptoms include coolant loss, hard starting, blue smoke on overrun
Rebuilt D5244T engines include upgraded EGR and injector components
items.cta: Compare rebuilt D5244T engine prices for your Volvo XC90

items.question: Is the Volvo XC90 T6 timing chain failure serious?
items.answer: Yes - timing chain tensioner failure on B6294T T6 engines can cause valve‑to‑piston contact, making engine replacement the only reliable solution once symptoms appear.
keyPoints: Rear‑mounted tensioner loses hydraulic pressure with age
items.cta: Get quotes for B6294T Volvo XC90 replacement engines

items.question: Are used Volvo XC90 engines reliable?
items.answer: Used XC90 engines can be reliable if sourced from verified UK breakers with compression test reports, though limited warranty terms mean rebuilt units offer better long‑term protection.
keyPoints: Used engines typically £850-£2,600 depending on code and mileage
Always request compression and leak‑down test documentation
D5244T and B6324S codes most available in UK breaker network
3‑month warranty standard on used units vs 12‑month on rebuilt
items.cta: Check used Volvo XC90 engine availability with test reports

items.question: Which engine code fits my Volvo XC90?
items.answer: Enter your registration above to instantly identify your XC90's exact engine code via DVLA data, ensuring compatibility before ordering a replacement unit from our UK specialist network.
keyPoints: DVLA lookup confirms engine code, fuel type and variant instantly
Common codes include D5244T, B6304T, B4204T, D4204T families
Engine code determines compatible rebuilt unit specifications
Incorrect code selection risks fitment issues and voided warranty
items.cta: Find your Volvo XC90 engine code with free registration lookup

items.question: Do you supply Volvo XC90 engines with fitting?
items.answer: Yes - EngineMarket's UK network offers supply & fit packages for Volvo XC90 engines, adding £650-£1,400 to the engine price for professional installation by Volvo‑trained technicians.
keyPoints: Fitting includes removal, installation, fluids and ECU coding
T8 hybrid fitting requires hybrid‑certified specialist technician
Nationwide coverage with road test included post‑installation
Book fitting after engine selection via EngineMarket platform
items.cta: Find Volvo XC90 engine supply & fit deals in your area

items.question: What warranty comes with a rebuilt Volvo XC90 engine?
items.answer: Every rebuilt Volvo XC90 engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a Volvomum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour at any UK workshop.
keyPoints: Warranty valid UK‑wide at any qualified garage or specialist
Covers manufacturing defects and premature component failure
Upgraded components addressing known failure points included
Warranty documentation provided with every rebuilt unit
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Volvo XC90 engines with 12‑month warranty

items.question: How long does Volvo XC90 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply‑only rebuilt XC90 engines typically dispatch within 2-5 working days, while supply & fit installations require 1-3 days workshop time depending on engine complexity and variant.
keyPoints: Common codes like D5244T often available for next‑day dispatch
T8 hybrid and V8 units may require 5-10 day lead time
Fitting time varies: 1 day for D4/T5, 2-3 days for T8/V8
Network specialists can advise exact turnaround for your variant
items.cta: Get quotes for Volvo XC90 engine replacement with delivery time

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Volvo XC90?
items.answer: Engine swaps on the XC90 are complex due to ECU coding, emissions compliance and AWD system integration - we recommend like‑for‑like replacement with a rebuilt unit for reliability and legality.
keyPoints: ECU adaptation required for any engine code change
Emissions compliance must match original variant for MOT
AWD system calibration specific to original engine torque output
Like‑for‑like rebuilt engines avoid legal and technical complications
items.cta: Compare like‑for‑like rebuilt engines for your Volvo XC90
